headline: Survey at 22% of target with strong KiuFunza momentum but critical regional gaps.
collection_status: 299 responses collected against 1,350 target (22.1% completion). Five regions (Lindi, Mara, Tanga, Kigoma, Mtwara) account for 78% of responses; five regions severely underrepresented. Current pace suggests 1,200-1,350 achievable by end of collection period if acceleration begins immediately.
key_findings: Expansion demand is dominant: 110 respondents (37%) prioritize expanding KiuFunza, with policy integration a close second (60 respondents, 20%), signaling stakeholder confidence in model scalability.

Teacher cohort is well-represented: 146 KiuFunza teachers (49% of sample) plus 73 head teachers provide credible ground-level feedback; volunteer and WEO participation (53 combined) ensures community perspective.

Scale readiness score of 2.7/5 contradicts expansion enthusiasm—suggests implementation capacity gaps despite appetite for growth; partner satisfaction (4.4/5) is solid but masking underlying systemic constraints.

Feedback themes align with data priorities: stakeholders cite need for teacher incentives, digital monitoring, expanded geographic reach, and government policy anchoring—all operationally feasible but resource-dependent.
flags: Regional coverage crisis: Rukwa (5), Manyara (4), and Singida (17) are critically undersampled; without targeted mobilization, regional representativeness will be compromised.
Scale readiness paradox: 2.7/5 readiness score suggests respondents doubt implementation capacity despite high expansion priority—risk of unrealistic commitments without infrastructure investment.
Research priority collapse: Only 6 respondents (2%) prioritized research despite KiuFunza's evidence-based positioning; indicates potential M&E or learning culture gap.
recommendation: Launch immediate regional catch-up campaign in Rukwa, Manyara, Singida, and Shinyanga with targeted partner outreach (target: 350+ responses in next 4 weeks). Simultaneously conduct rapid capacity diagnostic with 15-20 respondents to unpack the 2.7/5 readiness score and translate expansion demand into resource requirements for programme planning.
